response:
To find the probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A we define the difference variable D as X_B minus X_A. Since both variables are normally distributed their difference is also normal with mean equal to 75 minus 70 which is 5 and variance equal to 12 squared plus 10 squared which is 244. The standard deviation of the difference is the square root of 244 or approximately 15.62. We seek the probability that D is greater than zero. The z-score for zero is (0 minus 5) divided by 15.62 which equals roughly minus 0.32. Looking up this z-score in a standard normal table gives a cumulative probability of about 0.3745. The probability of being greater than zero is one minus this value or approximately 0.6255.

For the second part we must find the time t where performance levels match. The exponential function for Group A is 50 times e to the power of 0.02t. The logistic function for Group B is 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of minus 0.15 times (t minus 5). Setting these equal creates a transcendental equation that requires numerical methods for an exact solution. An explanation of this process shows that at t equals 10 the exponential value is roughly 61.07 while the logistic value is about 73.11. At t equals 15 the exponential value is roughly 74.59 and the logistic value is about 88.08. At t equals 20 the exponential value is roughly 90.25 and the logistic value is about 95.26. The curves appear to converge near t equals 22 where the exponential value is 94.3 and the logistic value is 97.3. Further refinement suggests the intersection occurs slightly before t equals 23. No closed form solution exists for this specific equation so we rely on iterative approximation. Nothing in the math forces an exact integer answer but the time is approximately 22.5 units. This process demonstrates how different growth models interact over time.
